pattern: Pre-order DFS tracks the maximum value seen on the path from the root; a node is good when its value is at least that running maximum.
hints: A node qualifies if no ancestor has a strictly larger value — equivalently, it is at least the maximum value encountered on the root-to-node path.
A pre-order traversal naturally carries the current path maximum as a parameter, and the root always qualifies since there are no ancestors.
Pass the running maximum downward: at each node compare its value to the maximum, increment a counter if it qualifies, update the maximum, then recurse into both children.

edge_inputs.description: mixed good and not-good nodes, four qualify
root's left subtree has a decrease then increase
single node — the root is always good
strictly decreasing path — only the root qualifies
strictly increasing path — every node qualifies
all nodes have the same value — every node qualifies
root with two children, both smaller — only root is good
negative values — a node with value -5 is good if all ancestors are also -5 or below

stress.description: large random tree with value range spanning positives and negatives
